title: WS-07 Test scaffolding, scenario corpora, and acceptance harness
description: Run scope: build deterministic scaffolding and high-coverage integration harnesses that increase development velocity while hardening explorer, X-Ray, replay, and observation work.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the repo has reusable fixture hosts, promoted scenario corpora, golden assertions, UI/layout coverage, and host acceptance families that run as ordinary local checks.

title: Build deterministic fixture-host and scenario-factory helpers for explorer, X-Ray, replay, and observation work
description: Run scope: factor shared deterministic test helpers out of one-off tests so future explorer, X-Ray, replay, and observation work can reuse a stable fixture substrate.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: reusable fixture helpers exist for the promoted host profiles and scenario families and later tests can build on them without copying setup logic.
close_reason: Added reusable deterministic test fixture roots, promoted host builders, and scenario-family helpers, and refactored explorer, retained-run, replay, X-Ray, and observation tests onto them.

title: Establish a promoted scenario corpus covering explorer, diagnostics, formatting, replay, and observation families
description: Run scope: define and retain a promoted local scenario corpus that gives future beads concrete, reusable evidence families rather than ad hoc formula strings.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: promoted scenario families are explicit, reusable, and referenced by local verification paths across the main product planes.
close_reason: Promoted explicit formula, formatting, and observation scenario corpora and wired local verification to reuse them across explorer, diagnostics, formatting, replay, and observation paths.

title: Add golden artifact assertions for capability snapshots, X-Ray projections, persisted documents, and compare outputs
description: Run scope: add golden or snapshot assertions for the main retained and inspection artifacts so structural regressions are caught early.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the main artifact families have golden assertions that fail when shape or interpretation-critical fields drift unexpectedly.
close_reason: Added golden artifact assertions for capability snapshots, retained X-Ray, persisted document round-trip invariants, and twin-compare outputs.

title: Add UI and layout harness coverage for resize, scrolling, focus, and keyboard flows
description: Run scope: add automated UI or shell-level coverage for layout and interaction behavior that is critical to explorer and X-Ray usability.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the repo has runnable coverage for resize, scrolling, focus routing, and keyboard-first flows in the promoted shell surfaces.
close_reason: Added runnable shell interaction harness coverage for promoted keyboard shortcuts, focus routing, resize invariants, and scroll-surface visibility.

title: Assemble fast and full host acceptance families across explorer, X-Ray, replay, and Windows-only compare
description: Run scope: turn the promoted scenario and fixture families into fast and full acceptance runs that can guide ordinary development and release confidence.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: host acceptance families exist with a fast local path and a fuller promoted path spanning the major product planes.
close_reason: Added fast and full host acceptance entrypoints over the promoted explorer, X-Ray, replay, retained-artifact, and compare families, and verified the fast family.

title: Split verification commands into dev-velocity smoke runs and deeper integration families
description: Run scope: keep verification fast enough for frequent use by splitting quick smoke families from slower integration and acceptance coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: local verification entrypoints clearly separate fast smoke use from deeper integration coverage.

title: WS-01 Desktop-first shell bootstrap and platform honesty
description: Run scope: stand up the desktop-first shell that will host formula entry, results, diagnostics, and visible product-honesty state. Do not close this epic on shell design notes alone.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the shell launches, the core regions exist, and host-profile or platform truth is visible from running code.
close_reason: The native desktop shell exists with formula, result, and diagnostics regions, and it visibly reports host-profile, packet-kind, and platform-gate truth from runtime code.

title: Replace the proof entrypoint with a desktop shell that exposes formula, result, and diagnostics regions
description: Run scope: replace the proof-only entrypoint with a real shell path and create visible regions for formula entry, result output, and diagnostics. This is product code, not a screenshot bead.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the shell launches, the three core regions exist, and later editor work can land on the running shell.
close_reason: Replaced the proof entrypoint with a native eframe-based desktop shell, added explicit formula/result/diagnostics regions, and verified the shell path with cargo test plus cargo run -- --shell-smoke.

title: Surface host-profile, packet-kind, and platform honesty in the shell
description: Run scope: expose the active host-profile, packet-kind, and platform or host limitations directly in the shell from executable truth.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the shell visibly reports host-profile and packet-kind state, and blocked hosts or platforms are explicit rather than implied.
close_reason: The native shell now shows the active OC-H0 host profile, packet-kind register, and explicit desktop-only platform gate from runtime code; cargo test and cargo run -- --shell-smoke both succeed.

title: WS-10 Windows observation and twin-oracle comparison
description: Run scope: make Windows OxXlPlay capture and honest twin-compare a real product lane once the retained and replay floors are ready.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: Windows observation capture, retained comparison, reliability labeling, and widening-pressure flow operate as one honest product lane.

title: Harden OxXlPlay capture-run integration and provenance or lossiness persistence in Observation artifacts
description: Run scope: keep the Windows capture lane honest by hardening capture-run integration and persisting provenance, projection, and lossiness markers into Observation artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: Observation artifacts preserve the promoted OxXlPlay provenance and lossiness facts needed for later comparison interpretation.

title: Expand twin-compare workbench surfaces with reliability badges and blocked-dimension explanations
description: Run scope: make twin compare readable in product terms by surfacing reliability, projection limits, and blocked dimensions directly in the compare workbench.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: compare surfaces show reliability and blocked-dimension truth directly from retained artifacts and capability facts.

title: Wire widening-request generation to concrete compare gaps and capability truth
description: Run scope: turn comparison gaps into explicit widening requests tied to real compare state and capability truth instead of ad hoc notes.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: widening requests are produced from real compare gaps with the governing capability and reliability context attached.

title: Add Windows-only comparison regression families over retained OxXlPlay fixtures and live-capture gating
description: Run scope: add regression families for the compare lane that can use retained OxXlPlay fixtures everywhere while keeping the live-capture gate Windows-only and explicit.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: compare regression families exist and keep Windows-only live-capture scope explicit.

title: WS-06 Replay capture and X-Ray surfaces
description: Run scope: emit replay outputs from retained runs, open replay from the product, and add X-Ray or diff surfaces over retained artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: retained runs emit replay outputs, replay opens from the product, and X-Ray or diff surfaces are reachable on real retained data.

title: Emit replay capture outputs from retained runs and open them from the host
description: Run scope: generate replay outputs from real retained runs and make the product open them with the current replay floor labeled honestly.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: retained runs produce replay outputs, the product can open them, and the surfaced replay floor is explicit.

title: Add X-Ray and diff surfaces over retained run artifacts
description: Run scope: expose retained artifact internals through X-Ray views and make diff surfaces reachable from the product.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: X-Ray opens on retained run artifacts and diff surfaces are reachable on real retained data.

title: WS-09 Replay substrate proving ground, semantic logging, and handoff foundations
description: Run scope: treat replay as the highest-risk proving lane by making the host operation model and retained replay substrate explicit instead of assuming it is already solved.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the replay-bearing operation model, substrate mapping, explicit seam pressure, and proving tests exist for the current honest lane floor.

title: Define the replay-aware operation model for edit-accept, manual, forced recalc, and future undo/redo or macro capture
description: Run scope: make the host-side operation model explicit for the actions that should later participate in reproducible replay, semantic logging, undo or redo, and macro-style capture.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the promoted host operations are classified into an explicit replay-aware model with clear retained consequences and non-assumptions.

title: Map retained OneCalc run artifacts onto OxReplay bundle, replay, diff, and explain inputs without local reinvention
description: Run scope: align retained OneCalc artifacts with the admitted OxReplay input surfaces and remove any remaining host-local reinterpretation of replay mechanics.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: retained runs map onto the current admitted OxReplay surfaces through explicit contracts and not through host-private reinterpretation.

title: Define semantic logging boundaries across OxFml, OxFunc, OxReplay, and host-owned retained actions
description: Run scope: make clear which semantic and operational facts belong to upstream libraries versus the host so replay-aware logging does not silently drift into private local doctrine.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: semantic logging boundaries and current seam gaps are explicit enough to drive implementation and upstream pressure without hidden local ownership drift.

title: Implement witness, explain, and handoff baseline flows over the current honest replay floor
description: Run scope: make witness, explain, and handoff flows operate over the current honest replay floor while keeping blocked dimensions explicit where the floor does not yet justify more.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the host can produce witness, explain, and handoff outputs for the admitted floor and blocked dimensions remain visible rather than silently hidden.

title: Add replay substrate proving tests across retained runs, reopened artifacts, and operation-model boundaries
description: Run scope: add proving tests that exercise the replay-aware operation model over retained and reopened artifacts so the high-risk lane becomes executable evidence rather than narrative.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: replay substrate tests exist for the promoted run families and fail when operation-model or retained-chain assumptions drift.

title: Generate structured upstream-pressure packets for unresolved replay seam gaps discovered by OneCalc
description: Run scope: ensure unresolved replay seam gaps discovered during proving work are captured as structured upstream pressure instead of normalized local divergence.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: unresolved replay gaps found by OneCalc generate explicit upstream-pressure artifacts or packets.

title: WS-11 Replay facade migration
description: Run scope: move OneCalc replay-aware capture and retained evidence projection onto the landed OxFml_V1 consumer replay surface while preserving retained identity and provenance truth.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: ordinary replay capture and retained projection use OxFml_V1 replay request/service/result objects, and replay provenance comes from canonical facade results rather than broad helper drilling.

title: Migrate ordinary replay capture and opening onto ReplayProjectionRequest, ReplayProjectionService, and ReplayProjectionResult
description: Run scope: move the ordinary OneCalc replay-capture and replay-open flow onto the landed OxFml_V1 consumer replay facade while preserving replay identity and projection truth. Acceptance: ordinary replay capture no longer depends on the historical broad helper path as its normal integration shape.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: ordinary replay capture and replay opening use ReplayProjectionRequest, ReplayProjectionService, and ReplayProjectionResult, rather than the historical broad helper path as the normal integration shape.

title: Consume canonical replay projection metadata in retained evidence, witness, and handoff flows
description: Run scope: wire retained evidence consumers to the replay projection metadata now published by OxFml_V1, including scenario alias, snapshot pin, registry, witness, and lifecycle fields where present. Acceptance: retained replay-aware flows consume canonical replay projection metadata rather than local private mappings.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: retained replay-aware evidence flows consume canonical replay projection metadata for alias, pin, registry, witness, and lifecycle truth where present, instead of local private mappings.

title: WS-05 Artifact spine and capability-snapshot implementation
description: Run scope: implement artifact identity, shared envelopes, lineage, and immutable capability snapshots in code.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the core artifact backbone exists in code and capability snapshots are emitted from real product state.

title: Implement core artifact envelope, lineage, and stable references in code
description: Run scope: implement shared artifact identity and envelope types in code and capture lineage or reference relationships explicitly.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: artifact identity and envelope types exist in code and later persistence or replay work can consume them directly.
close_reason: Added shared artifact envelope, stable artifact ref, and lineage types in code, and wired retained Scenario and ScenarioRun records to carry those envelopes and refs through real persistence and compare paths.

title: Emit immutable capability snapshots tied to retained runs and sessions
description: Run scope: generate immutable capability snapshots from the actual host environment and tie them to real runs or sessions.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: retained runs can reference immutable capability snapshots emitted from executable truth.
close_reason: Added immutable capability snapshot emission from executable truth, persisted snapshots alongside retained runs, and wired run envelopes to point back to the governing snapshot by stable ref.

title: WS-09 RTD subset and platform-gating rollout
description: Run scope: create the concrete RTD child beads once the extension path and platform truth are explicit.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: explicit RTD child beads exist with platform gates encoded.

title: Roll out concrete RTD child beads from the admitted extension subset
description: Run scope: derive the RTD child beads from the concrete extension path, keep the implementation within the admitted RTD subset, and encode platform gates explicitly.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: RTD child beads exist explicitly and remain tied to the admitted extension and platform subset.

title: Model RTD capability declarations and blocked-by-platform provider state over loaded extension roots
description: Run scope: extend extension-root loading so RTD capability declarations are preserved as explicit provider state, kept distinct from admitted host-managed function registration, and surfaced as blocked or unavailable by current platform/runtime truth rather than silently discarded. Acceptance: provider load results keep RTD declarations visible, blocked reasons remain explicit, and runtime truth can report RTD declaration state without implying admitted execution.

title: Implement Windows in-process RTD provider activation and update lifecycle for the admitted subset
description: Run scope: admit the narrow Windows RTD subset through the extension path, activate declared RTD providers through the Windows host contract, keep lifecycle and update packets explicit, and leave unsupported provider shapes visibly blocked. Acceptance: the host can activate the admitted Windows RTD provider subset, lifecycle/update state is executable and testable, and blocked RTD shapes remain explicit instead of being normalized away.

title: Implement Linux RTD activation registry and explicit host gate reporting
description: Run scope: provide the minimal Linux activation registry and host contract for the admitted RTD subset, or keep the path explicitly blocked where the current implementation cannot yet honor it. Acceptance: Linux RTD state is represented honestly in code and tests, with executable activation where admitted and explicit gates where the subset is still blocked.

title: WS-02 Formula editor and OxFml diagnostics integration
description: Run scope: implement real formula editing in the shell and integrate the OxFml edit and diagnostics path as the authoritative editor substrate.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: editing is real and keyboard-usable, OxFml edit packets are in the loop, and diagnostics are visible in the shell.
close_reason: The shell now supports keyboard-first formula editing, real OxFml FormulaEditRequest/FormulaEditResult flow, visible live diagnostics with spans, and runnable smoke verification for both valid and invalid formulas.

title: Implement formula buffer, cursor, selection, and keyboard-first editing in the shell
description: Run scope: build a real editor interaction model in the shell, including buffer state, cursor movement, selection, and ordinary keyboard editing.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the editor supports ordinary keyboard editing and exposes buffer, cursor, and selection state in code.
close_reason: The shell now uses a real keyboard-editable text editor, keeps explicit buffer/cursor/selection state in code, and exposes that state through the UI and shell-smoke output; cargo test and cargo run -- --shell-smoke succeed.

title: Wire OxFml FormulaEditRequest and FormulaEditResult through editor state
description: Run scope: translate editor state into OxFml edit requests, consume edit results, and keep the host side aligned with the upstream packet model.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the shell editor flows through the real OxFml edit-packet path and no local stand-in packet flow remains.
close_reason: The shell now drives OxFml FormulaEditRequest and FormulaEditResult through a runtime editor session, and shell-smoke prints the real edit-packet summary instead of a local stand-in edit flow; cargo test and cargo run -- --shell-smoke succeed.

title: Project OxFml diagnostics and spans into visible UI state
description: Run scope: render OxFml diagnostics, spans, or locations visibly in the shell and keep their provenance tied to the upstream diagnostic output.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: diagnostics update as the user edits, span or location information is visible, and the displayed state comes from real OxFml output.
close_reason: The shell now renders OxFml live diagnostics and span locations from the real FormulaEditResult, and verification covers both shell-smoke and an invalid-formula test case that surfaces diagnostic lines.

title: Add editor smoke verification for formula entry and diagnostics
description: Run scope: add the smallest honest verification path that proves formula entry, edit-packet flow, and diagnostics work together in the running shell.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: a runnable proof or test exists and fails if the real editor-plus-diagnostics integration is broken.
close_reason: Added a dedicated runnable editor diagnostic smoke path for an invalid formula, and it proves formula entry, real edit-packet flow, and live diagnostics together by emitting a nonzero OxFml diagnostic count; cargo test and cargo run -- --editor-diagnostic-smoke succeed.

title: WS-06 Engine X-Ray and mechanism inspection
description: Run scope: make Live Formula Semantic X-Ray the second major product perspective over the same scenario rather than a detached developer-only dump.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: parse, bind, eval, trace, and provenance truth are inspectable through a durable X-Ray surface tied to the active scenario and retained runs.

title: Define and implement the durable X-Ray panel structure for parse, bind, eval, trace, and provenance views
description: Run scope: implement the durable panel or drawer shape for X-Ray inspection so the mechanism view has a coherent product home inside the workbench.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the X-Ray surface has a stable panel structure with explicit sections for the promoted mechanism views.
close_reason: Added a durable left-side X-Ray drawer with parse, bind, eval, trace, and provenance sections, verified with cargo test -p dnaonecalc-host

title: Project parse, bind, evaluation, and trace artifacts into structured X-Ray view models
description: Run scope: convert the current runtime truths into structured X-Ray view models instead of ad hoc formatted strings so the mechanism layer can be rendered, tested, and retained honestly.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the X-Ray surface is driven by structured view models backed by real runtime data for parse, bind, evaluation, and trace artifacts.
close_reason: Replaced ad hoc X-Ray rendering with structured parse, bind, eval, trace, and provenance view models, verified with cargo test -p dnaonecalc-host

title: Surface provenance, capability, and host-driving context inside the X-Ray view
description: Run scope: connect mechanism inspection to the governing host-driving, provenance, and capability truth so the X-Ray can explain not just what happened but under what conditions it happened.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the X-Ray includes explicit provenance or context blocks tied to the active scenario or run rather than requiring users to infer the governing conditions.
close_reason: Expanded X-Ray provenance with host-driving packet kind, platform gate, capability floor, runtime class, and mode availability context, verified with cargo test -p dnaonecalc-host

title: Unify active-scenario and retained-run X-Ray opening paths around the same mechanism model
description: Run scope: keep active and retained X-Ray paths aligned by reusing the same mechanism model and view shape rather than growing separate shell-only and retained-only render paths.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: retained runs reopen into the same X-Ray model family used for active scenario inspection.

title: Add X-Ray golden and interaction coverage for promoted mechanism families
description: Run scope: add verification that the promoted X-Ray views remain structurally stable and still represent real mechanism truth across active and retained flows.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: promoted X-Ray families have golden or integration coverage that fails on view-model drift or missing mechanism truth.
close_reason: Added active and retained X-Ray golden coverage on top of the structured X-Ray models and existing interaction checks, verified with cargo test -p dnaonecalc-host

title: WS-11 Workspace management, Capability Center, scenario library, and acceptance
description: Run scope: complete supporting workspace and honesty surfaces once the main explorer, X-Ray, and replay lanes exist.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: workspace management, scenario library, acceptance evidence, and Capability Center operate as supporting product surfaces without crowding out the main workbench.

title: Implement workspace manifest UX and multi-document management without cross-instance semantics
description: Run scope: add workspace-level document management while preserving the OneCalc rule that documents remain isolated instances rather than workbook-like interacting sheets.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the workspace can group and reopen multiple OneCalc documents without implying cross-instance recalc semantics.

title: Rework the Capability Center into a persistent supporting surface with export, copy, and diff-target selection
description: Run scope: turn the Capability Center into the intended supporting honesty surface with export, copy, and diff-target behavior once the explorer shell has stabilized.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the Capability Center supports export, copy, and meaningful diff targeting while remaining a supporting surface rather than the main workbench.

title: Build scenario-library filters, promoted-family views, and lineage inspection over retained artifacts
description: Run scope: make the retained corpus browsable as a scenario library with promoted-family filters and lineage inspection rather than a recent-files list.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the scenario library can filter, inspect, and reopen retained families through explicit lineage-aware views.

title: Build acceptance evidence views and release-facing summaries from the host acceptance matrix
description: Run scope: surface acceptance evidence and promoted regression outcomes in product or release-facing summaries so the acceptance matrix becomes an ordinary operating surface.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: acceptance evidence can be inspected or summarized from the host acceptance matrix and promoted regression families.

title: Add workspace and scenario-library reopen regression coverage
description: Run scope: add regression coverage for workspace reopen, library filters, and lineage inspection so supporting surfaces remain trustworthy as the corpus grows.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: workspace and library reopen behavior is covered by runnable regression families.

title: WS-10 Workspace management and capability-center implementation
description: Run scope: implement multi-file workspace management for isolated OneCalc documents and surface immutable capability truth through a real capability center.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: multiple isolated documents can be managed together and the capability center reads real immutable snapshots.
close_reason: Workspace management and capability-center lanes are complete with real multi-document backend support and immutable capability snapshot UI/diff surfaces.

title: Implement multi-file workspace management for isolated OneCalc documents
description: Run scope: manage multiple isolated documents together without introducing cross-instance recalc or workbook semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: multiple isolated documents can be managed in one workspace and their retained truth stays separate.

title: Build the capability center and snapshot-diff UI over immutable capability snapshots
description: Run scope: display the active immutable capability snapshot in the product and provide a diff flow sourced from the same truth retained runs use.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the capability center reads real snapshots and supports snapshot diff without maintaining a second capability-truth model.
close_reason: Capability center now reads persisted immutable capability snapshots and diffs them in the shell without a second truth model.

title: WS-04 Driven-run comparison and H1 verification
description: Run scope: compare retained driven runs and keep the H1 path alive with a promoted smoke family.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: retained driven runs can be compared and the H1 path has a promoted smoke-family proof.
close_reason: Retained driven runs now compare through real code and the promoted H1 smoke family keeps the driven path verifiable.

title: Add retained version-to-version comparison for driven runs
description: Run scope: compare two retained driven runs for the same scenario and prepare the path later replay or twin-compare work will build on.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: two retained driven runs can be compared through real code and the output is usable by later compare surfaces.
close_reason: Added retained driven-run comparison in code, tightened ScenarioRun records with authored and evaluation truth, and verified version-to-version comparison over two real H1 runs.

title: Add a promoted H1 smoke family that proves the driven path stays runnable
description: Run scope: promote a small but meaningful H1 smoke family and use it to verify the driven path.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: a promoted H1 smoke family exists and catches regressions in retained or driven behavior.
close_reason: Added a promoted H1 smoke family script that runs the driven, retained, and compare smokes and fails on real regression markers across the H1 path.

title: WS-03 OxFunc surface and admitted-function policy integration
description: Run scope: consume the OxFunc library context in code, derive admitted-function labels, and surface the resulting function policy honestly in the running product.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: function-surface truth comes from OxFunc data in code, admitted labels are derived rather than hard-coded, and the shell can report the active function policy.

title: Consume OxFunc library context and derive admitted function labels in code
description: Run scope: load the current OxFunc context, apply admitted-function labeling in code, and make the resulting policy available to later editor and evaluation work.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the host consumes OxFunc context in code, admitted labels are derived in code, and downstream evaluation beads can consult the resulting surface.

title: Surface the active admitted function policy in the running shell
description: Run scope: project the active function policy into visible shell state so unsupported or provisional functions are explicit to the user.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the shell shows the current admitted function policy and does not imply unsupported functions are available.

title: WS-09 Extension ABI and provider-loading rollout
description: Run scope: create the concrete extension ABI and provider-loading child beads once the core runtime, shell, and capability substrate are stable enough to host them honestly.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: explicit extension child beads exist with dependencies and proofs wired, and the path no longer depends on chat memory.

title: Roll out concrete extension ABI and provider-loading child beads
description: Run scope: inspect the implemented runtime and capability substrate and create explicit child beads for ABI definition, provider loading, registration, and invocation.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: concrete extension child beads exist and are implementation-facing rather than document placeholders.
close_reason: Concrete extension child beads now exist for ABI, provider loading, and invocation over the current runtime and capability substrate.

title: Implement the admitted desktop extension ABI and exclusion register in code
description: Run scope: define the concrete desktop extension ABI types, admission gates, and exclusion register in code so later provider loading and invocation have one executable contract. Acceptance: extension ABI types exist in code, unsupported hosts/capabilities are explicitly blocked, and the result is ready for provider loading work.
close_reason: The admitted desktop extension ABI and exclusion register now exist in code with executable validation and explicit blocked capability reasons.

title: Load extension provider manifests and capability declarations from a local extension root
description: Run scope: load provider manifests from a configured local extension root, validate them against the admitted ABI, and surface provider capability declarations as executable host truth. Acceptance: provider manifests load through the new ABI contract and invalid providers are rejected explicitly.

title: Invoke admitted extension providers through the host evaluation path and report provider failures
description: Run scope: execute admitted extension providers through the host path, keep provider failure reporting explicit, and surface provider availability honestly in runtime truth. Acceptance: extension invocation works for the admitted subset and failure states stay visible rather than being normalized away.

title: WS-11 Editor facade migration
description: Run scope: move OneCalc edit, diagnostics, completion, help, and carrier-validation integration onto the landed OxFml_V1 consumer editor surface without rebuilding local semantic ownership.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: ordinary editor interactions use OxFml_V1 consumer editor objects and canonical interaction results, and OneCalc no longer hand-stitches edit/completion/help flow from historical direct-consumer calls.

title: Migrate formula edit and diagnostics flow onto EditorEnvironment, EditorDocument, and EditorEditService
description: Run scope: move the ordinary edit and diagnostics path onto the landed OxFml_V1 consumer editor facade, preserving immutable or snapshot-oriented editor state and incremental reuse truth. Acceptance: the host no longer manually assembles FormulaEditRequest and adjacent context as its ordinary edit path.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: ordinary edit and diagnostics flow uses EditorEnvironment, EditorDocument, and EditorEditService, while immutable or snapshot-oriented editor state and incremental reuse truth remain visible.

title: Migrate completion, signature help, and current help onto canonical editor interaction results
description: Run scope: move completion, signature help, and current function help onto OxFml_V1 editor interaction results and canonical function-help packets, retiring routine local OxFunc metadata stitching in the editor path. Acceptance: ordinary help and completion rendering consume the consumer editor facade directly.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: ordinary completion, signature help, and current help rendering consume canonical editor interaction results and function-help packets, without routine local OxFunc metadata stitching in the editor path.

title: Align conditional-format carrier validation with the documented OxFml_V1 editor consumer story
description: Run scope: refactor the isolated conditional-format validation path onto the intentional OxFml_V1 placement for carrier-specific validation, whether as specialized editor-service operation or explicitly adjacent semantic operation. Acceptance: the carrier-validation path is migrated and no longer reads as unresolved spillover from the historical surface.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the isolated conditional-format carrier-validation path is migrated onto the intentional OxFml_V1 placement and no longer reads as unresolved spillover from the historical seam.

title: WS-11 Seam verification and cleanup
description: Run scope: verify the runtime, editor, and replay migration as one coherent OxFml_V1 seam uptake, remove remaining ordinary direct-consumer substrate usage, and leave a clean verified migration frontier for later product work.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: migrated runtime/editor/replay proofs all pass, remaining ordinary direct-consumer OxFml usage is removed from host code, and later breadth work is no longer deepening the superseded seam.

title: Refresh runtime, editor, and replay smoke proofs against the OxFml_V1 consumer surface
description: Run scope: refresh and extend the current runtime, editor, replay, retained-run, and capability proofs so they exercise the migrated OxFml_V1 consumer surface rather than the historical direct-consumer path. Acceptance: the migrated seam is covered by runnable tests or smokes that prove preserved behavior and retained truth.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: runnable tests or smokes prove the migrated runtime, editor, replay, retained-run, and capability flows still behave honestly on the OxFml_V1 consumer seam.

title: Audit remaining direct-consumer OxFml usage and confine any unavoidable advanced access to bounded tests only
description: Run scope: audit the codebase for remaining historical direct-consumer OxFml usage, remove ordinary host-code reliance on it, and confine any unavoidable advanced access to explicit bounded tests or upstream mismatch probes only. Acceptance: ordinary OneCalc host code reads as an OxFml_V1 consumer integration, with any exceptional advanced access isolated and justified.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: ordinary OneCalc host code reads as an OxFml_V1 consumer integration, with any unavoidable advanced OxFml access confined to explicit bounded tests or mismatch probes only.

title: WS-01 Runtime dependency integration and seam boundary
description: Run scope: integrate real OxFml, OxFunc, and OxReplay dependencies into the workspace and establish the host-side runtime adapter boundary. This epic must land code, not seam-summary docs.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the workspace builds against the real upstream dependencies, and the host crate exposes a concrete adapter boundary for later editor and evaluation work.
close_reason: The workspace now builds against real OxFml, OxFunc, and OxReplay dependencies through dnaonecalc-host, and the host exposes a concrete runtime adapter boundary for later editor and evaluation work.

title: Integrate OxFml, OxFunc, and OxReplay as real workspace dependencies
description: Run scope: update manifests to reference the actual upstream repos or packages, make the host crate compile against them, and remove any local-only assumption that pretends the dependencies are already present.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: manifests reference the real dependencies, cargo build or check succeeds, and there is no local fake integration path.
close_reason: Workspace now builds against real OxFml, OxFunc, and OxReplay dependencies via the dnaonecalc-host crate; cargo test and cargo run both succeed.

title: Establish the runtime adapter layer and host-profile substrate in code
description: Run scope: implement the host-side adapter layer that later editor and evaluation beads will call, and encode host-profile plus packet-kind truth in code rather than notes.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: runtime adapter types exist in code, host-profile and packet-kind truth are executable types, and later work can call them directly.
close_reason: Added runtime adapter types, explicit OC-H0/OC-H1/OC-H2 host-profile ladder, executable packet-kind register, and a reusable adapter API that already calls the real OxFml parse path and dependency probe; cargo test and cargo run succeed.

title: WS-08 Windows observation capture and twin compare
description: Run scope: integrate Windows-only Excel observation through OxXlObs and build the twin-compare path with provenance, lossiness, and reliability made explicit.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: Windows observation capture works, Observation and Comparison artifacts are produced from real runs, and the product can open the twin-compare path.
close_reason: Windows observation capture, twin compare, and widening-pressure output are all implemented over real retained evidence.

title: Integrate Windows OxXlObs capture-run and persist Observation artifacts with provenance and lossiness
description: Run scope: call the Windows capture path, retain Observation artifacts from real captures, and keep platform gating plus lossiness explicit.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the Windows capture path is integrated and Observation artifacts carry provenance plus lossiness classification.
close_reason: Windows OxXlObs capture-run is integrated, real Observation artifacts persist with provenance and explicit lossiness, and capability truth now reflects Excel-observed availability.

title: Build Comparison artifacts and twin-compare views over retained run versus Observation
description: Run scope: compare a retained OneCalc run against a Windows Observation and present the result in the product with honest reliability labeling.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: Comparison artifacts are generated in code and the twin-compare view opens on real data.
close_reason: Comparison artifacts now persist for retained run versus Observation, twin-compare views open on real data, and reliability plus projection limits are surfaced explicitly.

title: Emit widening-request output when comparison envelope blocks intended validation
description: Run scope: detect when the current compare envelope is too narrow and produce widening-pressure output from the real compare path.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: widening-request output is generated from real compare state and blocked validation dimensions are explicit.
close_reason: Real compare state now emits an OxXlObs-targeted widening handoff with explicit blocked validation dimensions and capability-gated lineage.

title: WS-08 Driven single-formula host, retained runs, persistence, capability snapshots, and ScenarioCapsule
description: Run scope: strengthen the durable H1 host and artifact spine before serious replay or observation widening.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the driven host, retained scenarios and runs, capability snapshots, persistence invariants, and ScenarioCapsule transport work as one coherent durable spine.

title: Consolidate the driven single-formula host session model and explicit recalc triggers around the retained scenario spine
description: Run scope: keep edit-accept, manual, and forced recalc behavior coherent around one durable H1 session model that later replay work can consume honestly.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the retained H1 host model and explicit recalc triggers are unified around one coherent session spine.

title: Strengthen retained scenario and run reopen invariants across active and reopened H1 flows
description: Run scope: harden the reopen path so retained scenarios and runs preserve the invariants that later X-Ray, replay, and compare work depend on.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: retained scenarios and runs reopen with explicit invariants preserved and failures surface as real regressions.

title: Make capability snapshots govern retained runs, documents, and workspace-facing artifacts consistently
description: Run scope: ensure capability snapshot truth governs the durable artifact spine consistently instead of being a sidecar fact used only in isolated flows.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: retained run, document, and related artifact paths all preserve and reopen the governing capability snapshot truth consistently.

title: Expand ScenarioCapsule inventory, integrity checks, and attachment preservation for the current artifact spine
description: Run scope: harden ScenarioCapsule export and intake so it remains a trustworthy transport for the current OneCalc artifact spine.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: ScenarioCapsule export and intake preserve the intended artifact inventory, capability refs, and integrity checks for the promoted retained families.

title: Add end-to-end retained-host integration coverage across recalc, reopen, persistence, and capsule flows
description: Run scope: add integration coverage that proves the retained H1 spine works across recalc, reopen, persistence, and capsule transport as one system.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: there is runnable integration coverage for the promoted retained-host families and it fails on spine regressions.

title: WS-04 Driven single-formula host and retained runs
description: Run scope: implement the H1 driven host model, retain real scenarios and runs, and reopen retained runs without drifting toward worksheet semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the H1 path exists in code, retained scenario and run handling are real, and retained runs reopen successfully.
close_reason: The H1 driven single-formula host model, retained Scenario and ScenarioRun handling, and retained-run reopen path now exist in executable code.

title: Implement the H1 driven host model and recalc context
description: Run scope: extend the first vertical slice into the H1 driven model with explicit recalc context while staying inside single-formula scope.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the H1 path exists in code, recalc context is explicit, and the implementation stays within admitted single-formula scope.
close_reason: Added an explicit H1 driven single-formula host model, explicit recalc context, edit/manual/forced recalc entry points, and runnable H1 smoke plus tests through the public OxFml host path.

title: Persist Scenario and ScenarioRun identities for real runs and reopen them
description: Run scope: persist scenario and scenario-run identity fields for real H1 runs and reopen them through the product path.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: real runs produce retained scenario and scenario-run records and those runs reopen through the product path.
close_reason: Added retained Scenario and ScenarioRun record types, file persistence, a reopen path that reconstructs the driven H1 host, and runnable retained-run smoke plus tests.

title: WS-06 Witness, explain, and handoff generation
description: Run scope: build witness, explain, and handoff generation on top of replay or diff state while sourcing gates from real capability truth.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: witness or explain output and handoff packets are generated from real retained state and remain honestly gated.

title: Implement witness and explain generation over retained replay or diff state
description: Run scope: generate witness and explain output from retained replay or diff state and keep blocked dimensions visible rather than invented.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: witness or explain output is generated from real retained state and blocked dimensions remain explicit.

title: Implement handoff packet generation gated by capability truth
description: Run scope: generate handoff packets from retained evidence and gate them with immutable capability truth rather than local prose.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: handoff packets are generated from real retained evidence and capability snapshots control what they may claim.

title: WS-07 Conditional-formatting subset and compare labeling
description: Run scope: implement the admitted isolated conditional-formatting subset and keep it distinct from ordinary formatting in X-Ray and compare-adjacent surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the admitted CF subset exists in code and the product distinguishes formatting from conditional formatting honestly.

title: Implement the admitted isolated conditional-formatting carrier subset
description: Run scope: add only the admitted isolated-instance conditional-formatting subset and keep unsupported CF behavior explicitly blocked.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the admitted CF subset exists in code and unsupported CF scope remains visibly unavailable.

title: Surface formatting versus conditional-formatting truth distinctly in X-Ray and compare views
description: Run scope: project the distinct formatting and CF planes into X-Ray and compare-adjacent surfaces so omitted dimensions remain explicit.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the UI distinguishes formatting from CF truth and unsupported dimensions remain visibly unavailable.

title: WS-05 Formula/function explorer UX and result surface stabilization
description: Run scope: stabilize the primary explorer workbench so formula editing, result reading, effective-display inspection, and shell layout all behave like a serious interactive product.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the explorer-first shell is stable under resize and ordinary use, result and context surfaces stay visible, and the promoted explorer flow has runnable regression coverage.
close_reason: Explorer layout, dock behavior, scrolling, keyboard routing, help surfaces, array previews, and regression coverage are all landed and verified.

title: Restructure the primary workbench layout so formula, result, diagnostics, and context regions remain stable under resize
description: Run scope: refactor the shell layout into a durable explorer information architecture that keeps formula authoring, result inspection, diagnostics, and context panes visible without panel collision.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the explorer shell preserves the core regions under common desktop window sizes and no major pane disappears under routine resize behavior.
close_reason: Implemented stable explorer layout with right-side inspector and verified with cargo test -p dnaonecalc-host

title: Rework the Capability Center into a non-obscuring supporting surface with preserved dock behavior
description: Run scope: stop the Capability Center from crowding out the explorer path by turning it into a supporting dock or drawer surface with preserved openness and size behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the Capability Center no longer hides the result surface by default and its docked behavior is stable across repeated resize and toggle cycles.
close_reason: Moved Capability Center into a supporting inspector dock with explicit show-hide state and verified with cargo test -p dnaonecalc-host

title: Add independent scrolling and long-content handling for result, diagnostics, and capability surfaces
description: Run scope: ensure long result text, diagnostics, and capability content remain readable through explicit scroll behavior instead of clipping or panel collapse.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: long explorer outputs can be inspected fully in place and the shell exposes explicit scrolling where overflow occurs.
close_reason: Added independent scrolling for result, diagnostics, and capability surfaces and verified with cargo test -p dnaonecalc-host

title: Strengthen function-help, completion, and effective-display presentation in the explorer workbench
description: Run scope: improve the placement and readability of function-help, completion, and effective-display truth so the main workbench behaves as a formula/function explorer before deeper mechanism work begins.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the promoted explorer surfaces show function-help, completion, and effective-display truth clearly without requiring a secondary developer view.
close_reason: Rendered structured completion and help surfaces and promoted effective display presentation, verified with cargo test -p dnaonecalc-host

title: Harden keyboard-first explorer commands, focus routing, and panel toggles
description: Run scope: keep the explorer-first shell keyboard-usable by making focus, evaluate, and panel-toggle flows deterministic and non-fragile.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: core explorer actions are keyboard-usable and focus does not jump unpredictably across panes during ordinary workflows.
close_reason: Added keyboard shortcuts for evaluate and panel toggles with explicit editor focus routing, verified with cargo test -p dnaonecalc-host

title: Add explorer UX regression coverage for resize, visibility, and scrolling behavior
description: Run scope: add runnable regression coverage proving that resize, panel docking, scrolling, and result visibility stay intact as the explorer shell evolves.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: automated regression checks fail if the explorer shell again hides result or capability content under resize or overflow.
close_reason: Added regression-state tests and layout invariants for explorer visibility, dock state, and scroll behavior, verified with cargo test -p dnaonecalc-host

title: Display result arrays in the explorer result surface up to a practical preview size
description: Run scope: render evaluated array results in the main result surface instead of collapsing them to a scalar-style summary, while keeping the preview bounded to a practical size so the explorer remains usable for ordinary formulas.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: array-valued results render their visible contents directly in the main result surface up to a practical bounded preview size, with truncation or overflow made explicit rather than hiding the array contents.
close_reason: Array-valued explorer results now render a bounded main-surface preview with explicit truncation, with runtime and shell regression coverage.

title: WS-03 Completion and current help integration
description: Run scope: integrate deterministic completion and the current help or signature surfaces into the real editor flow without overclaiming upstream maturity.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: completion and current help surfaces are visible in the editor and remain aligned with actual upstream support.

title: Integrate deterministic completion proposals into the real editor flow
description: Run scope: request deterministic completion from the current upstream path and project proposals into the real editor flow.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: completion proposals come from the real upstream-backed path and are visible in the editor.

title: Integrate current function and signature help surfaces into the editor
description: Run scope: show the currently available help or signature data in the editor while keeping missing upstream detail visibly absent or provisional.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: current help or signature data is visible in the editor and the UI does not claim richer help than upstream truth provides.

title: WS-03 First dependency-backed formula evaluation slice
description: Run scope: deliver the first real formula-entry to evaluation to result slice through OxFml and OxFunc.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: an admitted formula can be entered and evaluated through the intended dependencies, the shell shows the result honestly, and the whole path has runnable verification.

title: Wire evaluate action through OxFml and OxFunc for admitted formulas
description: Run scope: connect the shell's evaluate action to the real OxFml and OxFunc-backed runtime path and ensure results are not synthesized locally.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: evaluate action calls the upstream-backed runtime and the shell can execute at least one admitted formula honestly.

title: Render evaluated result and effective-display status in the shell
description: Run scope: show the returned result and current effective-display truth in the running shell rather than in proof-only logs.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the shell visibly renders the result from the real evaluation path and updates when real runs occur.

title: Add runnable verification for formula entry, evaluation, and result rendering
description: Run scope: add the smallest useful proof that the first vertical slice really works from formula entry through result rendering.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: a runnable proof or test exists, it exercises the real shell path, and it fails on real integration regressions.

title: WS-12 Extension ABI, RTD, XLL packaging, and later OxVba pressure
description: Run scope: land the later extension lane after the explorer, X-Ray, replay, and observation floors stabilize, while keeping the high-complexity ABI work explicit and honest.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the admitted extension subset, RTD lifecycle, packaging parity, and later OxVba pressure are all explicit and tested without widening OneCalc into worksheet REGISTER.ID/CALL semantics.

title: Freeze the admitted OneCalc extension ABI subset register and safety model for RTD, XLL, and .so bring-up
description: Run scope: define the admitted ABI subset and safety model precisely enough that later implementation work does not drift into a looser Excel-like add-in story.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the admitted ABI subset and safety model are explicit enough to govern later implementation and verification work.

title: Implement provider discovery, validation, enablement, and load-state reporting with platform gates
description: Run scope: implement the extension lifecycle states for discovery, validation, enablement, and runtime load with explicit platform-gate truth.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: provider discovery and load-state reporting preserve the distinct states of discovered, admitted, enabled, and active.

title: Implement the admitted RTD lifecycle and host contract with unsupported-platform blocking explicit
description: Run scope: implement the admitted RTD lifecycle for supported hosts while keeping unsupported platforms and semantic gaps explicitly blocked.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the admitted RTD lifecycle works for supported hosts and blocked hosts remain visibly blocked rather than implied.

title: Add packaging and conformance harnesses for Windows XLL and Linux .so parity over the same ABI claims
description: Run scope: prove that Windows XLL and Linux .so packaging preserve the same declared ABI behavior through conformance-oriented verification.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: conformance or packaging harnesses exist for Windows XLL and Linux .so paths and tie back to the same admitted ABI claims.

title: Capture OxVba-driven extension pressure and unresolved upstream seams without normalizing local divergence
description: Run scope: record extension-lane gaps that require OxVba or other upstream changes through explicit pressure surfaces rather than local private contracts.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: unresolved extension-lane seams discovered during bring-up generate explicit upstream pressure instead of local drift.

title: WS-11 Runtime facade migration
description: Run scope: move ordinary and driven OneCalc execution from direct SingleFormulaHost/provider/query-bundle assembly onto the landed OxFml_V1 consumer runtime facade while keeping host policy above OxFml.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: ordinary and driven runtime entry use OxFml_V1 consumer runtime objects, pinned provider/query truth stays explicit, and the historical direct-consumer runtime assembly path is no longer the ordinary host path.

title: Migrate ordinary direct evaluation onto RuntimeEnvironment, RuntimeFormulaRequest, and RuntimeFormulaResult
description: Run scope: replace the current direct evaluate_formula path with the landed OxFml_V1 consumer runtime facade, preserving explicit provider-plus-pin, typed query-bundle, volatile inputs, and returned-value truth. Acceptance: ordinary host evaluation no longer manually assembles SingleFormulaHost, InMemoryLibraryContextProvider, and TypedContextQueryBundle as its ordinary path.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: ordinary host evaluation uses RuntimeEnvironment, RuntimeFormulaRequest, and RuntimeFormulaResult, with explicit provider-plus-pin, typed query, and volatile inputs preserved and no ordinary SingleFormulaHost/provider/query-bundle assembly left in the direct evaluation path.

title: Migrate driven single-formula and retained H1 execution onto RuntimeSessionFacade
description: Run scope: replace the local driven-host wrapper over SingleFormulaHost with the landed RuntimeSessionFacade while preserving recalc triggers, volatile inputs, retained scenario-run identity, and retained provenance. Acceptance: H1 driven execution and retained-run production use the consumer session facade as the ordinary repeated-execution path.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: driven single-formula execution and retained H1 run production use RuntimeSessionFacade as the ordinary repeated-execution path while preserving recalc triggers, volatile inputs, and retained provenance truth.

title: Retire remaining ordinary runtime substrate imports and normalize runtime integration vocabulary to OxFml_V1
description: Run scope: remove remaining ordinary host-code dependence on historical direct-consumer runtime substrate imports, keep frozen upstream packet names, and leave any truly necessary advanced access bounded and explicit. Acceptance: runtime-facing host code reads as a consumer::runtime integration rather than a historical root-surface composition.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: ordinary runtime-facing host code reads as an OxFml_V1 consumer::runtime integration, with remaining advanced runtime access removed or isolated explicitly outside ordinary host flow.

title: WS-05 Document persistence and ScenarioCapsule transport
description: Run scope: implement isolated document round-trip and ScenarioCapsule export plus intake on top of the retained-artifact spine.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: one document round-trips through the declared format, required invariants survive, and ScenarioCapsule export plus intake work on real data.

title: Implement SpreadsheetML round-trip for isolated OneCalc documents
description: Run scope: save and reopen one isolated OneCalc document through the declared persisted document format without widening into workbook semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: a document can be saved and reopened through the declared format and remains within isolated OneCalc scope.

title: Preserve retained identity and formatting invariants across document round-trip
description: Run scope: verify that retained identity and currently admitted formatting invariants survive save and reopen.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: required identity and formatting invariants survive round-trip and the proof fails on real regressions.

title: Implement ScenarioCapsule export and intake with lineage and capability refs intact
description: Run scope: export one scenario with selected retained artifacts as a ScenarioCapsule, re-import it, and preserve lineage plus capability refs.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: ScenarioCapsule export and intake work on real scenario data and do not lose lineage or capability truth.

title: WS-07 Formatting and effective-display implementation
description: Run scope: keep returned presentation hints and host style state separate and render effective display for the admitted formatting subset.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: effective display is rendered from real code and the formatting-plane split is explicit in product state.

title: Separate returned presentation hints from host style state in code
description: Run scope: represent returned presentation hints separately from host style state and keep that distinction explicit in code and UI state.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: the two planes are separate in code and later rendering can consume them without ambiguity.

title: Render effective display for the admitted formatting subset
description: Run scope: render effective display from the implemented formatting plane and expose the current display state visibly in the product.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: effective display is visible in the product and derived from the implemented formatting plane.

title: WS-10 Scenario library, acceptance, and upstream-pressure rollout
description: Run scope: create the concrete scenario-library, acceptance-matrix, and upstream-pressure child beads once the live product surfaces they depend on are in place.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: explicit child beads exist for the later scenario-library and acceptance path, all tied to real product surfaces.

title: Roll out concrete scenario-library and promotion child beads from live replay, compare, persistence, and workspace pressure
description: Run scope: use the now-live replay, compare, persistence, and workspace surfaces to create explicit child beads for scenario promotion, library UX, and acceptance evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: concrete scenario-library child beads exist and depend on live product features rather than planning prose.

title: Roll out concrete acceptance-matrix and upstream-pressure child beads from widened evidence
description: Run scope: create the detailed acceptance and upstream-pressure child beads once promoted-scenario and widened-evidence paths exist.
acceptance_criteria: Evidence: concrete acceptance and upstream-pressure child beads exist and identify the evidence they will consume.

title: Build the promoted scenario index from retained runs, compare evidence, witness output, and handoff state
description: Run scope: derive a concrete promoted-scenario index from the retained run, replay, compare, witness, handoff, and workspace surfaces already live in OneCalc. Acceptance: the product has a code-backed promoted-scenario index source with stable row identity and enough evidence links to drive later scenario-library UX honestly.

title: Implement scenario-library filters and saved views over host, replay, witness, handoff, and platform relevance
description: Run scope: project the promoted-scenario index into filterable and savable library views using the actual retained evidence already produced by the host. Acceptance: scenario-library filters and saved views are backed by real retained fields and do not invent metadata outside the live product surfaces.

title: Implement scenario selection detail and lineage-trail actions over replay, compare, witness, and handoff artifacts
description: Run scope: build the selection-detail model for promoted scenarios, including lineage and launch actions that open the retained replay, compare, witness, and handoff surfaces already produced by OneCalc. Acceptance: scenario selection detail is backed by real artifact links and lineage, and the available actions correspond only to surfaces that actually exist for the selected scenario.

title: Build host acceptance-matrix rows from promoted scenarios and capability truth
description: Run scope: derive explicit acceptance-matrix rows from the promoted scenario index and immutable capability truth so host claims, replay availability, compare availability, witness presence, and handoff readiness become ordinary product evidence. Acceptance: acceptance-matrix rows are generated from live retained/product truth and do not rely on separate planning metadata.

title: Run the promoted scenario regression family and persist acceptance summary output
description: Run scope: execute the promoted scenario family as a routine regression lane and persist the resulting acceptance summary so the matrix is backed by actual repeated runs rather than static rows. Acceptance: a promoted scenario regression run produces acceptance summary output tied back to the promoted rows it covers.

title: Emit routine upstream-pressure packets from promoted evidence, compare widening, and seam blockers
description: Run scope: turn promoted scenario evidence, compare widening requests, and blocked acceptance dimensions into routine upstream-pressure packets so seam pressure becomes an ordinary output of product execution. Acceptance: upstream-pressure packet generation is driven from live promoted evidence and identifies the scenario and blocker context it came from.
